question: What is this repository's memory?
title: Collective memory
summary: SaaS Idea Manager is a small PHP app—but this guide is the preserved understanding of six months of AI-assisted building.

scenes.headline: The code is the artifact. This guide is the memory.
scenes.body: SaaS Idea Manager stores SaaS product ideas in SQLite for one operator. That is the software. What you are reading is something different: the reasoning, rejections, assumptions, and session discoveries that normally disappear when a chat closes.

facts.label: Without VibeKB
facts.text: The code survives. The conversations disappear. Six months later nobody completely understands why.

facts.label: With VibeKB
facts.text: Understanding becomes part of the repository—captured while building, not reconstructed afterwards.

facts.label: This sample
facts.text: A real project shape with a fictional-but-honest six-month evolution story. Every decision file maps to something the app actually does or deliberately does not do.

scenes.label: The workflow this demonstrates
scenes.description: This loop ran dozens of times while building SaaS Idea Manager.
scenes.insight: VibeKB is not the guide you are reading—it is the habit of capturing reasoning at each step so the guide can exist.

steps.title: Names the intent
steps.text: “I need a place to stash weekend SaaS ideas before I forget them.”
steps.example: Captured in project.json and guide chapter 1—before any CRUD existed.

steps.title: Implements the feature
steps.text: Generates PHP, SQLite schema, and templates in a single session.
steps.example: Week 1: ideas list and create flow shipped fast.

steps.title: Records the why
steps.text: Decision, rejected alternatives, and alignment warnings land in .vibekb/ the same week.
steps.example: No auth—not an oversight. Documented boundary.

steps.title: Reads the guide next time
steps.text: Future sessions start from repository knowledge instead of guessing intent.
steps.example: Month 2: PostgreSQL suggestion deferred because the decision file already exists.

steps.title: Understands immediately
steps.text: A new operator—or you, six months later—opens the guide instead of re-interviewing the code.
steps.example: You are here. That is the point.

scenes.label: What you will walk through
scenes.description: Each chapter is a layer of preserved understanding—not a documentation tour.

steps.title: How it grew
steps.text: Day 1 to month 6: guide size, features, and knowledge captured alongside code.

steps.title: Why it is shaped this way
steps.text: Architecture evolution, feature reasoning, and decision history with rejected paths.

steps.title: What to know before editing
steps.text: Developer warnings, lessons learned, and AI handoff notes for the next session.
